args.prompt:
Create a Python script named predict_stock.py for Alphabet Inc. under finance/Alphabet. The script should:
1) Use the ticker GOOGL.
2) Load historical daily closing prices for the past month. Prefer a local CSV file if one exists in the same folder (e.g., historical_prices.csv with columns Date, Close); otherwise fetch the past month of data using yfinance.
3) Build a simple time-series forecasting model in Python using only standard scientific libraries (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn, matplotlib). A linear regression on day index is acceptable.
4) Predict the next 5 trading days closing prices.
5) Save predictions to finance/Alphabet/prediction.csv with columns: Date, Historical_Close, Predicted_Close, Set.
   - For historical rows, Predicted_Close can be blank/NaN and Set='historical'.
   - For forecast rows, Historical_Close can be blank/NaN and Set='prediction'.
6) Produce a line chart that shows historical closing prices and predicted values with different colors/styles and a legend/title.
7) Save the chart as finance/Alphabet/prediction.jpg.
8) Include a main() entry point and helpful console prints.
9) Ensure the script creates the finance/Alphabet directory if needed and runs end-to-end when executed.

Write clean, production-quality code with imports, comments, and error handling.

Create a Python script named predict_stock.py for Alphabet Inc. under finance/Alphabet. The script should:
1) Use the ticker GOOGL.
2) Load historical daily closing prices for the past month. Prefer a local CSV file if one exists in the same folder (e.g., historical_prices.csv with columns Date, Close); otherwise fetch the past month of data using yfinance.
3) Build a simple time-series forecasting model in Python using only standard scientific libraries (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn, matplotlib). A linear regression on day index is acceptable.
4) Predict the next 5 trading days closing prices.
5) Save predictions to finance/Alphabet/prediction.csv with columns: Date, Historical_Close, Predicted_Close, Set.
   - For historical rows, Predicted_Close can be blank/NaN and Set='historical'.
   - For forecast rows, Historical_Close can be blank/NaN and Set='prediction'.
6) Produce a line chart that shows historical closing prices and predicted values with different colors/styles and a legend/title.
7) Save the chart as finance/Alphabet/prediction.jpg.
